title: Light Peppery Middle Eastern Chickpeas Tagine
description: A bright, peppery chickpea tagine that balances cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika with a velvety drizzle of clotted cream, and can be made dairyfree with coconut cream.

ingredients.name: clotted cream
ingredients.notes: optional; replace with coconut cream for dairy‑free

instructions: Rinse soaked chickpeas and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot or tagine over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and a pinch of freshly ground black pepper; cook spices for 30 seconds to bloom.
Add chickpeas, lemon juice, and vegetable broth. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook 25–30 minutes until chickpeas are tender and sauce thickens.
Stir in clotted cream (or coconut cream), allowing it to warm through but not boil, to retain its silky texture.
Taste and season with salt, adjusting lemon juice if needed; add a pinch of cayenne if extra heat is desired.
Remove from heat, stir in chopped fresh cilantro and a few drops of lemon juice if extra brightness is desired.
Serve hot with warm flatbread or over couscous, garnished with extra cilantro.
